problem_statement: Mfidel atomicity is a hard invariant, but SDK, kernel, and runtime surfaces need a typed receipt that records substrate digest, row and column bounds, exact fidel preservation, no-normalization proof refs, and cross-runtime fixture evidence state before parity claims.
success_criteria: the MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt validates against a repository-local JSON schema
the receipt records row bounds 1 through 34 and column bounds 1 through 8
the receipt preserves representative fidel sequences exactly across source and preserved fields
the receipt denies Unicode normalization, decomposition, recomposition, forbidden internal-letter modeling, lossy transliteration as canonical storage, and fidel-splitting tokenization
the receipt records Python, TypeScript, and Rust fixture refs while keeping runtime parity as AwaitingEvidence until live fixture evidence exists
missing or drifted grid refs, receipt refs, evidence refs, guard flags, and summary counts fail closed with explicit validator errors
non_goals: no fidel splitting into internal shape or sound parts
no tokenizer, OCR, transliteration, SDK, or kernel runtime implementation
constraints: each fidel remains one atomic symbol
sound overlays remain metadata only and never structural decomposition
cross-runtime parity remains AwaitingEvidence until Python, TypeScript, and Rust fixtures are bound
effect-bearing follow-on work still requires UAO, Phi_gov, security review, rollback or recovery evidence, and verification
evidence_required: focused pytest receipt for validator behavior
SDLC requirement, design, and security review validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
